involvements.title: LifeTagger Inc.
involvements.body: LifeTagger is a proximity based tool that allows users to connect with the location surrounding them, strengthening customer engagement of several local businesses. My experience with the company over 4 years includes working with libraries such as React.js, Node.js, Android App Development, Flutter, and Parse Cloud in order to build solutions on the web, in mobile apps, and a backend server connecting it all together. Using these skills, I was able to not only increase customer engagement with registered companies, but also aid the victims of COVID-19 and Hurricane Ida with real-time statistics and relief resources.

involvements.title: Wisconsin Autonomous
involvements.body: Wisconsin Autonomous is a student-driven organization at the University of Wisconsin - Madison working on generating new technology through several competitions centered around automation. Currently, Wisconsin Autonomous is competing in the AutoDrive Challenge II hosted by SAE International at the University of Michigan with aspirations of creating an L4 autonomous vehicle in the coming years. On the controls subteam, I have used libraries such as ROS2 to create a high-level planner that works along with mid-level planners and a controller to ensure the accurate path-planning of the Chevrolet EUV.

involvements.title: Average Warriors Fan
involvements.body: Average Warriors Fan is a chatbot built upon TensorFlow, NLTK, Scikit Learn, and Numpy is an extremely basic chatbot that is capable of basic conversation. Eventually as the project continues to be built, it will develop the personality of a fan of the Golden State Warriors. A simple front-end UI is under construction, but the open source code is available to view on my GitHub.

involvements.title: RoboLoCo
involvements.body: RoboLoCo is a robotics organization based at Loudoun County, VA participating in the First Robotics Competition. During my time here for the Rapid React season, as the software leader I led the development of robot ready code as well as ensuring that the team had the adequate equipment to scout others well. After being selected as alliance captains and competing in the playoffs of the district competitions we were able to make District Championships. Here, we were named finalists for the Engineering Inspiration award which led us to compete at the 2022 World Championship in Houston, TX.

involvements.title: Wando Advanced Robotics (Phoenix)
involvements.body: Coming in as a freshman in high school, I joined this FIRST Robotics Competition (FRC) team looking to learn. Quickly, as I learned more, I supervised the development of vision software, mobile scouting apps, and electrical boards for competitions and ded the development of competition-ready robot code.
